# G5#3: PermissionService.spec AGENT:* singleton cross-pollution under workers:1

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-08 during PR #10933 (Phase 3 unit-row re-add) CI run [25524203756](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25524203756/job/74915736779). Originally classified as G5#3 in the [#10924 G5 triage matrix](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10924#issuecomment-4400451588). Triage hypothesis was "auto-resolve post-G4-merge"; falsified empirically by the unit-row first activation in CI.

Skip-guarded in PR #10933 commit `8e6c3fd78` per the established pattern; this ticket tracks the proper investigation + fix.

## The Problem

Test `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs:165` flakes with:

```
expect(node.type).toBe('BroadcastSentinel');
  Expected: "BroadcastSentinel"
  Received: undefined
```

The test pre-seeds `AGENT:*` as a `BroadcastSentinel` via `GraphService.upsertNode`, then asserts the type survives `grantPermission`. Empirically `node.type` is `undefined` — meaning either:
- A sibling spec re-upserted `AGENT:*` with a different shape (no `type` field), OR
- The singleton `GraphService` was re-initialized between the seed and the read, dropping the in-memory state

`workers:1` substrate amplifies cross-pollution. Per grep, **4 specs touch `AGENT:*`**:
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/MailboxService.spec.mjs`
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/WriteSideInvariant.spec.mjs`
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs`
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs` (this one)

Whichever sibling runs first and writes `AGENT:*` without a `type` field clobbers the BroadcastSentinel shape.

## The Architectural Reality

- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs:165-181` — the flaky test
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.mjs#upsertNode` — last-writer-wins on the `id` key; updates fields per the upsert payload
- 4 spec-files all write `AGENT:*` to the same singleton GraphService
- `workers:1` serializes execution → cumulative state pollution

## The Fix (TBD via investigation)

Two candidate paths:

1. **Spec-level**: each spec uses a UNIQUE `AGENT:*` analog (e.g., `AGENT:*permission-test`, `AGENT:*mailbox-test`) so writes don't collide on the same node ID
2. **Symmetric beforeEach+afterEach**: each spec resets `AGENT:*` to a known-clean shape at the start AND end of each test (per `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup` pattern from earlier session)

Investigation needed to determine which sibling clobbers first + which fix shape minimizes blast radius.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** Empirically reproduce the flake locally with `WORKERS=1`
- [ ] **(AC2)** Identify which sibling spec(s) write `AGENT:*` without preserving `type`
- [ ] **(AC3)** Implement chosen fix path (per-spec namespaced ID OR symmetric reset)
- [ ] **(AC4)** Remove the NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI guard added in PR #10933 commit `8e6c3fd78`
- [ ] **(AC5)** Verify PermissionService.spec runs deterministically across 5 consecutive `npm run test-unit` invocations on CI substrate

## Out of Scope

- Refactoring GraphService.upsertNode to require explicit type field (architectural change beyond spec-isolation scope)
- Migrating GraphService to per-test instances

## Avoided Traps

- **Increasing `retries: 2 → 5`**: papers over without addressing root cause
- **Skip-guard as permanent solution**: applied as immediate ship-the-PR move + tracked here for proper fix

- **Surfacing CI run:** [25524203756](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25524203756/job/74915736779)

- **Triage correction:** [#10924 comment 4401547656](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10924#issuecomment-4401547656)
- **Sibling state-pollution patterns:** G5#2 (KBRecorderService — singleton-data), #10934 (FileSystemIngestor — singleton SQLite-close), #10935 (TransportService residual)
- **Substrate config:** `test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs` `workers: 1` in CI
- **Skip-guard commit:** `8e6c3fd78` on PR #10933

- 2026-05-08T06:48:41Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `2e23dca` - "fix(test): root-cause fix — stop closing GraphService singleton in FileSystemIngestor.afterAll (#10934)

Path B per the iteration cycle on PR #10933:

The cascading flakes (FileSystemIngestor / GraphService.spec / Database.spec /
PermissionService) all traced back to FileSystemIngestor.spec.afterAll closing
the singleton SQLite (`GraphService.db.storage.db.close()` + `GraphService.db = null`
+ `_initPromise = null`) under workers:1 — sibling specs' SDK lazy re-init
breaks once the singleton is torn down, surfacing different consumer-site
errors per CI run.

Replaced the close+null with state-clear via the existing `clear()` chain:
- `GraphService.db.nodes.clear()` / `edges.clear()` / `vicinityLoadedNodes.clear()`
- `GraphService.db.storage.clear()`

The connection stays open across spec boundaries; FileSystemIngestor's test
artifacts (graph nodes/edges) are dropped via clear() so they don't leak into
subsequent specs. Single-commit fix vs N skip-guards for cascading consumers.

Reverted now-unnecessary skip-guards on three downstream consumers since the
root cause is fixed:
- FileSystemIngestor.spec: removed describe-scope + beforeEach + test-body skip-guards
- Database.spec: removed describe-scope skip-guard (G6 residual auto-resolves)
- PermissionService.spec: removed test-body skip-guard (singleton stays initialized)

Skip-guards retained on KBRecorderService (different singleton: kb_query_log)
and TransportService (different substrate: HTTP bind), since those flakes do
NOT cascade through GraphService close — separate root causes tracked under
#10936 + #10935 respectively.

Closes #10934 if CI confirms the cascade is broken; #10937 + #10938 likely
also auto-resolve via the same fix."
- 2026-05-08T06:49:26Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10933
- 2026-05-08T06:54:24Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `5cf8e99` - "fix(test): extend Path B to Server.spec + GraphService.spec afterAll; re-add G5#3 skip-guard (#10934, #10937)

Iteration on PR #10933 c.2e23dcaf7 — CI residual showed:
- FileSystemIngestor.spec:126 STILL flaky (sibling close-singleton from
  Server.spec.afterAll OR GraphService.spec.afterAll cascades into FSI's
  beforeAll/test before SDK lazy re-init can recover)
- PermissionService.spec:165 flaky (data-pollution shape, NOT addressed
  by Path B's connection-close fix)

Per `feedback_lead_role_decision_thresholds`: scope-correctly Path B in
one push instead of iterating. Empirical grep showed 4 specs close the
singleton; my prior c.2e23dcaf7 only fixed FileSystemIngestor.

This commit:
- Server.spec.afterAll: replace close+null with clear() chain (matches
  the FileSystemIngestor pattern)
- GraphService.spec.afterAll: same
- PermissionService.spec:165: re-add NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI guard pointing to
  #10937 (G5#3 — AGENT:* data pollution, distinct from connection-close)

WakeSubscriptionService.spec: untouched. Its destroy+immediately-re-init
pattern in beforeAll (lines 55-71) is intentional for force-fresh isolation
+ the immediate initAsync at line 71 leaves the singleton properly initialized
for downstream specs. The `_initPromise = null` + `db = null` calls there
are safe because they're paired with re-init.

Server.spec.beforeEach also destroys (lines 50-58) but each test that runs
calls initAsync internally — leave those alone; only fix afterAll.

Iteration cost retro: should have applied Path B to all 4 specs in c.2e23dcaf7
instead of just FileSystemIngestor. The grep audit was the right empirical
verification step — should have run it BEFORE pushing the partial fix."

- 2026-05-08T11:05:09Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `5f1ac1f` - "test(ci): add skip-guards for residual Bucket G flakes (#10939)

Addresses Gemini's PR #10953 Cycle 1 review (commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBpwL4A).
The PR body claimed skip-guards were in place but the diff was workflow-only —
the skip-guards themselves were missing. CI's `unit` row failed accordingly.

Adds NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI test.skip() at the top of each named flake's body, citing
the tracking ticket so removal is mechanical when the proper fix lands:

- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec.mjs:78
  → #10946 (synthesis-write race post-#10940 engine=hybrid fix)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/KBRecorderService.spec.mjs:92
  → #10936 (kb_query_log singleton-data pollution)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:159
  → #10941 (workers:1 close-singleton residual)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:174
  → #10941 (same root-cause class as :159)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs:182
  → #10937 (AGENT:* singleton-data pollution under workers:1)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/IssueService.spec.mjs:254
  → bucket G #10924 (GraphqlService mock-pollution residual; sub-ticket
  TBD — same root-cause class as #10942 PullRequestService:250 but distinct
  surface. Cite epic for now; sub-ticket if root cause diverges)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.spec.mjs:24
  → #10935 (HTTP bind race residual post-#10930 fix)

GraphService.spec.mjs:108 and :136 already had the bucket-G3 skip-guard from
prior cycles; left untouched. All 7 new guards follow the existing canonical
pattern (`test.skip(!!process.env.NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI, 'CI-skip: <reason> (#N)')`)
established in #10907/#10921/#10928.

Per Gemini's review §Test-Execution & Location Audit + Required Actions, this
closes both bullets:
1. Skip-guards added for the 4 PR-body-named flakes
2. Skip-guards added for the 4 additional flakes Gemini caught in the CI run
   (GraphService:158/172, IssueService:253, TransportService:23)

- 2026-05-08T12:43:49Z @tobiu referenced in commit `98897fc` - "feat(ci): re-add unit suite to matrix post-Bucket-G substrate (#10939) (#10953)

* feat(ci): re-add unit suite to matrix post-Bucket-G substrate (#10939)

Bucket A-F audit (#10903) closed via per-bucket fixes (#10907 / #10921 /
#10910 / #10919 / #10920). Bucket G (#10924) substrate fixes landed via
PR #10940 (TestLifecycleHelper primitive + daemon-spec migration) plus
its follow-up #10944 (HealthService sqlite-vec artifact cleanup).

Residual Bucket G flakes remain tracked in their own sub-tickets — all
skip-guarded via NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI:
- #10941 — GraphService.spec:107 residual flake (hypothesis-2 falsified
  empirically; investigation tilts toward hypothesis 1 hook-scope or
  hypothesis 3 SDK lazy re-init contract)
- #10936 — KBRecorderService.spec:91 (kb_query_log singleton-data
  pollution, distinct root cause class)
- #10937 — PermissionService.spec:181 (AGENT:* singleton-data pollution)
- #10946 — DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec:77 (synthesis-write race
  unmasked by the engine='neo' to engine='hybrid' fix in #10940)

Empirical validation: 5-iteration AC4-strict run on origin/dev at
a771afdb0 produced identical composition each iteration (16 hard fail
+ 4-5 flaky + 3 did-not-run + 1056 passed, with the flake set above
deterministic 4/5 plus SessionSummarization:114 intermittent 2/5 which
is bucket-A skip-guarded in CI). FileSystemIngestor.spec (#10934) and
Database.spec (#10938) — the original Bucket G G6 cascade surfaces —
appear in zero failure / flake / did-not-run lists across all 5 runs.

Workflow edit: matrix `suite: [integration]` -> `suite: [integration,
unit]`. Comment block updated to reflect Bucket A-G substrate state and
the canonical NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI bucket-skip pattern (env unchanged from
prior workflow row).

Predecessor PRs that cycled and closed Drop+Supersede per
substrate-scope-restraint discipline:
- PR #10933 (5 cycles) - skip-guard whack-a-mole pre-substrate-fix
- PR #10943 (2 cycles) - same pattern, narrower scope

Both demonstrated empirically that workers:1 substrate flakes do not
converge linearly via skip-guards alone. Substrate fix landed first
(PR #10940); this PR is a clean workflow-line addition gated on the
substrate state, not iterative skip-guard cycling.

* test(ci): add skip-guards for GraphService.spec:242 + :284 residual flakes (#10939)

CI run after `5f1ac1fa9` surfaced 2 additional GraphService.spec hard failures
that were pass-on-retry-flaky in my local 5-run baseline but flipped to
hard-fail under CI's specific spec-interleaving order:

- `GraphService.spec.mjs:242` ('should recover from boot-time identity cache
  race (stuck vicinity cache)') → workers:1 close-singleton residual (#10941)
- `GraphService.spec.mjs:284` ('should lazy-load topology for getContextFrontier
  when frontiers drop out of cache') → same root-cause class as :242 + :107
  + :158 + :172 (#10941)

Both tests follow the same vicinity-cache + lazy-load-from-SQLite pattern
that exposes the close-singleton-then-reuse race tracked in #10941. Skip-guard
cites the existing tracker — same removal mechanic as the prior 5 GraphService
guards (#10941 covers all 5 surface variants).

This is the second iteration on the Phase 3 re-add post-#10940 substrate. Per
`feedback_substrate_scope_restraint`: NOT cycling on iterative skip-guards via
the same PR (predecessor #10933/#10943 cycle pattern). Each commit on this PR
addresses a specific empirical CI signal — distinct from the iterative
whack-a-mole shape that triggered Drop+Supersede on the prior cycle.

Co-Authored-By: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>

* test(ci): add skip-guards for GraphService.spec:315 + :356 (#10939)

Gemini's PR #10953 Cycle 3 review flagged 2 more GraphService.spec hard
failures from CI on commit 4c2987c4c — both same workers:1 close-singleton
residual root-cause class as :107/:158/:172/:174/:242/:284 (all under #10941).

Per @tobiu's directive: keep iterating skip-guards until CI is green; ~95%
working in the GH workflow with some skipped tests is the canonical pattern
(established #10907/#10921/#10928 + cohort). Drop+Supersede was an
over-application of `feedback_substrate_scope_restraint` for unit-row re-add
work specifically — substrate-scope-restraint applies to substrate-fix
iteration cycles, not to skip-guard application on already-substrate-fixed
work.

- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:316
  ('should execute getPaths and lazy-load recursively across deep dependencies
   when RAM cache is missed') → #10941
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:357
  ('should automatically execute LRU garbage collection Native Graph footprints
   when maxGraphNodes is exceeded') → #10941

PR #10953 reopened (was closed Drop+Supersede prematurely). This commit
continues the iteration.

- 2026-05-08T16:45:09Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2405528` - "feat(ai): migrate memory-core/Server to extend BaseServer + beforeToolDispatch hook (#10965) (#10977)

PR5 of the M2 series — final per-server consumer migration. Memory Core
is the most complex (580-line original; identity resolution, sibling
concurrency, wake substrate, custom boot order, identity-spoof guard).

memory-core/Server.mjs reduced 580 → 417 lines (~28% reduction; less
than other migrations because of irreducible per-server complexity:
resolveStdioIdentity, bindAgentIdentity, logSiblingConcurrency, +
custom boot order with stdio-identity-resolution between dependent-
services and healthcheck per #10249).

BaseServer extension (load-bearing for this migration):
- New optional override hook `beforeToolDispatch({toolName, args})`
  fires BEFORE the healthcheck gate. Throws bubble to outer CallTool
  try/catch and route to formatToolError (NOT formatHealthError,
  semantic distinction matters). Default no-op.
- Memory Core uses it for AuthMiddleware.validateNoIdentitySpoof per
  ticket #10145 — must fail-fast on identity-spoofing requests before
  any other processing.

memory-core's boot() override (preserves all original semantics):
1. loadCustomConfig
2. WakeSubscriptionService.init() — pre-mcpServer per #10437
3. createMcpServer (override chains super + CoalescingEngineService.
   addMcpServer for experimental neo-wake-substrate broadcast)
4. InferenceLifecycleService.ready + SessionService.ready
5. Stdio identity resolution + HealthService.setStdioIdentityState +
   wake-subscription auto-bootstrap (fire-and-forget IIFE per #10438
   single-error-boundary discipline) — BEFORE healthcheck so the
   boot snapshot reflects bound identity state per #10249
6. runHealthcheckAndLogStatus + logSiblingConcurrency
7. connectTransport + logIdentityStatus (stdio only)

Hooks used:
- getServerMetadata (with experimental neo-wake-substrate capability)
- getToolService, getHealthService, getHealthExemptTools
- wrapDispatch (RequestContextService.run binding for stdio identity)
- beforeToolDispatch (AuthMiddleware identity-spoof guard)
- buildRequestContext (SSE per-request OIDC binding)
- onSessionClosed (SSE: queueSummarizationJob + CoalescingEngine cleanup)
- logStartupStatus (Memory Core flavored output)
- createMcpServer (override chains super for CoalescingEngineService bind)

Helpers preserved (memory-core-private):
- resolveStdioIdentity, bindAgentIdentity (with #10241 retry pattern)
- logIdentityStatus, logCollectionStats, logSiblingConcurrency

Tests:
- BaseServer.spec.mjs: 27 → 28 unit tests; 1 new for beforeToolDispatch
  (verifies hook fires BEFORE health gate, throws route to
  formatToolError NOT formatHealthError)
- 258 existing memory-core unit specs pass (5 pre-existing Bucket G
  flakes unrelated to migration: GraphService #10941, PermissionService
  #10937, SessionSummarization)

Refs #10965. M2 per-server migration sequence COMPLETE: PR1 scaffold
(#10966 merged), PR2 knowledge-base (#10973 approved), PR3 github-workflow
(#10974 approved), PR4 neural-link + boot() seam (#10975 approved), PR5
memory-core (this), PR6 file-system (#10976 in review).

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"

- 2026-05-09T22:35:47Z @tobiu closed this issue
